data.title: Corcept Rockets 48% after FDA Lifyorli Approval — What Does This Mean for CORT Stock?
data.description:
Shares in biopharma company Corcept Therapeutics climbed as high as 48% to $48.35 following U.S. approval of Lifyorli for treating certain ovarian-type cancers. H.C. Wainwright believes the move could enable the California-based company to finally diversify beyond Korlym.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Both Lifyorli (relacorilant) and Korlym (mifepristone) are used for patients with Cushing's syndrome, a condition where the body makes too much of the stress hormone, cortisol. However, while Korlym aims to fight high blood sugar caused by that excess cortisol, Lifyorli is designed to block the cortisol's harmful effects more selectively.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lifyorli for use in combination with chemotherapy drug nab-paclitaxel to treat adults whose ovarian-type cancer has stopped responding to standard platinum-based chemotherapy. Such patients are expected to have undergone between one and three prior therapies, including bevacizumab, before qualifying to use the newly approved combination.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Oil at $150 Will Trigger a 'Steep Recession,' Warns BlackRock CEO
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) CEO and co-founder Larry Fink has warned that oil rising to $150 could lead to a "stark and steep recession." Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, have surged 26% to $96 since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Fink noted that it is too early to determine the outcome of the war, although he expects one to two scenarios to unfold. The first is for Iran to be integrated into the international community following the end of the war, which would send oil below its pre-war level.

Strait of Hormuz Tensions Keep Oil Elevated

If the first scenario doesn't play out, the second scenario is much less rosy, with Fink calling for a recession and "years of above $100, closer to $150 oil, which has profound implications in the economy."

While traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has slightly picked up in recent days, it's still well below the rate seen before the start of the war. Iran has continued to reiterate that it will attack any U.S.-linked vessel crossing through the passageway and rejected an initial U.S. proposal to end the war, citing the demands as "excessive."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: CD Projekt Stock (OTGLY) Jumps Despite AI Game Development Integration
data.description:
CD Projekt (OTGLY) stock was up on Wednesday alongside news that the video game publisher has started implementing AI into its game development process. A new management report from the company noted that CD Projekt Red has started to prototype and test AI solutions for The Witcher 4 and Cyberpunk 2.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report from CD Projekt claimed that this AI technology is being used to create more realistic non-player characters (NPCs). Specifically, it's dedicated to adjusting the movement of large crowds of NPCs to make them appear more realistic to players. CD Projekt also said that this is one of roughly a dozen projects that its AI team is working on.

While CD Projekt stock is up today, the company could face backlash over its decision to include AI in the development of its games. Generative AI has received pushback from gamers, who don't want developers to replace human artistic visions with those generated by AI. This has resulted in terms like "AI slop" being applied to games that use the technology.

CD Projekt Stock Movement Today

CD Projekt stock was up 1.32% on Wednesday but was still down 3.57% year-to-date. The stock has also risen 17.26% over the past 12 months.

OTGLY stock movement today saw some 1,090 shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 1,240 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: IPO Prospect Databricks Buys Two Startups to Power New AI Security Platform
data.description:
Databricks, a leading data and AI company, has acquired two cybersecurity startups, Antimatter and SiftD.ai, to underpin the launch of its new AI security platform, Lakewatch. The move comes shortly after the company secured a $5 billion funding round, strengthening its position to invest in new capabilities and scale its platform.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Databricks Launches Lakewatch AI Security Platform

In a March 24 announcement, Databricks, best known for its cloud data analytics platform, unveiled Lakewatch, a new AI security platform that represents the company's strategic entry into cybersecurity. The platform combines Databricks' large-scale data storage capabilities with traditional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) functions, such as threat detection and investigation. 

Powered by AI agents running on Anthropic's Claude AI models, the system enables more advanced, automated security analysis. A Databricks spokesperson stated that Lakewatch positions Databricks at "the nexus of data platforms, AI agents, and cybersecurity, meeting enterprise demands for tools that can match the speed and sophistication of modern threats."

To build Lakewatch, Databricks integrated technologies and talent from two strategic acquisitions: Antimatter and SiftD.ai. The company acquired Antimatter in an undisclosed deal that closed last year but was only disclosed now, while the SiftD.ai acquisition came together rapidly over recent weeks, closing on March 23.

Databricks' Strategic Acquisitions Strengthen Security Expertise

According to sources familiar with the matter, both acquisitions were designed to bring critical cybersecurity expertise into Databricks' expanding security division. Antimatter was developing a sophisticated "data control plane" technology that enables the safe deployment of AI agents around sensitive data through advanced policies, including redaction, tokenization, and context boundaries. 

The deal likely included intellectual property as well. Founded by UC Berkeley security researcher Andrew Krioukov, Antimatter had raised $12 million in 2022 in a round led by New Enterprise Associates. Meanwhile, SiftD.ai brought deep expertise from Splunk veterans who created the company's Search Processing Language and built its search infrastructure for large-scale threat detection. 

The startup was notably young, having launched its collaborative notebook product only in November 2025. While Databricks declined to specify exactly how many employees it acquired from the deals, the AI company confirmed that key personnel from both startups joined the organization to strengthen its security capabilities for Lakewatch. Krioukov now leads the Lakewatch development team, bringing his cybersecurity research background directly into Databricks' product development.

What Is The Best AI Stock To Buy Now? 

While Databricks is still a private company ahead of a potential initial public offering (IPO) in 2026, investors interested in AI stocks can consider top players such as Nvidia (NVDA), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and Amazon (AMZN), all rated Strong Buys by Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: M&A News: Home Depot Picks Up Mingledorff's, Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Notches Up
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has long had designs on the professional market. It provides a welcome hedge against conventional economic slowdown, and opens up a new revenue stream that is a little more secure. And Home Depot took another big step to secure the pro market, buying Mingledorff's to augment its pro-focused lineup. Investors were reasonably pleased, and gave Home Depot shares a fractional boost in Wednesday morning's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mingledorff's is a wholesale dealer in HVAC equipment—heating, ventilation and air conditioning—that has 42 locations over the span of five states. Much of its business is located in the southeastern United States, reports note, and HVAC distribution represents a roughly $100 billion total addressable market.

Terms of the deal were not disclosed. However, reports suggest that this deal will not impact Home Depot's expected return to a 2x leverage ratio by the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2027. And, once that point is reached, Home Depot will likely restart its share buyback plans. In the end, Home Depot will have better access to the professional market and deliver further value in an attempt to get, or even stay, ahead of its competitors in the field.

12 New Stores This Year

Home Depot also plans to open an array of new stores in 2026 at a rate of roughly one per month on average. These 12 stores will be coming to a handful of states. States on the roster include Arizona, Arkansas, California, Florida, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas and Utah. Interestingly, Texas will get the most stores with four states, Florida will get two, and each other state will get one.

Home Depot revealed these plans not long after its earnings reports made it pretty clear that it is still pulling in customers, and at a rate better than expected. The new stores should not only make it more accessible to normal customers, but also to professionals, and result in improved revenue going forward.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 8.62% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $423.11 per share implies 27.63% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

SpaceX's Blockbuster IPO

An exact date for the SpaceX IPO hasn't been set, but Elon Musk has been racing towards the share sale in recent months, making management changes at the space company and

data.title: 'Don't Sleep on This': Microsoft Stock Gets the Thumbs up From Bank of America
data.description:
Investors might have cooled on Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stock in recent months; the shares have been in a downtrend since peaking in October, with concerns around capex and competition weighing on the stock, among more broader worries of an AI market bubble.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, Bank of America analyst Tal Liani doesn't believe the tech giant's value proposition is any less potent.

The analyst thinks Microsoft's edge comes from leveraging AI across both infrastructure and applications. Its Azure cloud platform provides the computing power and data backbone needed for enterprise AI workloads, while core software offerings such as Microsoft 365, Dynamics, GitHub, and Windows are embedded in daily workflows, supporting adoption and increased usage.

Looking ahead over the next few years, Liani sees three key debates that will ultimately shape how the risk–reward story unfolds.

The first revolves around the durability of Azure's growth. Its AI-related backlog has expanded significantly, with RPO (remaining performance obligations) reaching about $625 billion, up 109% year over year, but the key question is how quickly that contracted demand can be recognized as revenue. In the near term, growth is constrained by limited GPU supply, the pace of AI-optimized data center buildouts, power bottlenecks, and the need to allocate compute to Copilot, MAI, and OpenAI workloads, leaving Azure's trajectory closely tied to how quickly infrastructure capacity can scale. However, Liani believes demand is "real and durable," with supply – not demand – acting as the constraint. As projects like the Fairwater AI data center and other infrastructure initiatives come online, RPO conversion should accelerate, supporting Azure's continued leadership, although he notes that near-term free cash flow margins are likely to face pressure from elevated capex.

The second debate centers on Microsoft's OpenAI partnership and whether it strengthens long-term value or introduces execution and concentration risk. The updated structure, including a $250 billion OpenAI commitment and Microsoft's 27% stake, raises questions about the durability of its API and inference exclusivity, particularly as Microsoft no longer controls where models are trained. There are also risks stemming from OpenAI's large multi-vendor compute commitments, expected to exceed $1.4 trillion over eight years, along with a step-up in payments beginning in calendar year 2027, which could create concentration risk if monetization lags. The partnership also impacts Microsoft's earnings, with OpenAI-related losses flowing through GAAP results. That said, Liani believes the risk appears manageable, supported by "durable monetization" from API and inference usage and Azure's broad demand base, which helps offset periods of under-consumption.

The final debate is the biggest one of all – what AI actually represents. Is it a bubble, a disruptive force reshaping the software layer, or the early stage of a long, capital-intensive platform shift? And where does Microsoft fit within that evolution?

Investor concern has grown alongside the rapid rise in AI spending, given the unprecedented scale of capex and uncertainty around how agentic AI could reshape traditional software. The key question is whether the current investment will generate sustainable returns or eventually pressure Microsoft's core applications. Liani's view here is that AI represents a long-term platform shift rather than a bubble, with an investment cycle defined by upfront capex and later monetization. Microsoft is well positioned through Copilot and MAI, which integrate AI into products like M365, GitHub, Azure, and Security, reinforcing rather than displacing its software ecosystem, deepening workflow integration, and supporting "long-term returns" despite near-term pressure on free cash flow.

So, what does this ultimately mean for investors? Liani assigns MSFT stock a Buy rating and a $500 price objective, implying the stock will gain 35% in the months ahead. (To watch Liani's track record, click here)

The Street's average price target is even more exuberant; at $583.68, the figure makes room for one-year returns of ~58%. On the rating front, based on 33 Buys vs. 3 Holds, the consensus view is that this stock is a Strong Buy. (See Microsoft stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: HOOD Stock Skyrockets 8% after Robinhood Tries to Save It from Its Scary Crash with $1.5 Billion Buyback Plan
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD) is launching a massive $1.5 billion plan to buy back its own shares to stop a painful drop in its stock price. The company announced the move late Tuesday, March 24, 2026, as it tries to win back investors. After being one of the most popular stocks during the crypto boom, HOOD stock has lost more than 50% of its value since Bitcoin (BTC-USD) hit its peak in early October. HOOD stock jumped 8% in early trading today.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Large Buyback Plan Supports Shareholder Value

The new program adds over $1.1 billion to the money Robinhood already had set aside to buy its own stock. By purchasing these shares, the company reduces the total number of shares available, which can make each remaining share more valuable. Robinhood expects to carry out this plan over the next three years, starting right away in the first quarter of 2026.

This move shows that the board of directors believes the stock is currently too cheap. While the company is not forced to buy a set amount every month, having this $1.5 billion ready gives them a way to fight back against the bears who have been selling the stock off for months.

New Funding Deals Support Company Cash Flow

To make sure it has enough cash to grow, Robinhood also grew its credit line with major banks. Its subsidiary, Robinhood Securities, signed a deal led by JPMorgan (JPM) to increase its available credit to $3.25 billion. There is even an option to push that number as high as $4.875 billion if the company needs more liquidity.

This extra money gives Robinhood a safety net as it deals with the cooling crypto market. Since the company makes a lot of its money from people trading coins like Bitcoin, the recent crash in crypto prices have hurt its bottom line. This new funding ensures that Robinhood can keep running smoothly even if trading stays slow for a while.

Crypto Slowdown Creates Major Market Pressure on HOOD

Robinhood was one of the hottest stocks last year because everyone wanted to trade digital coins. However, as the crypto boom faded, so did interest in the HOOD stock. The shares rose about 1.4% in late trading after this news came out, but they still have a long way to go to recover from their recent scary crash.

Investors are now watching to see if this buyback plan is enough to turn things around. While the company is using its own cash to support the price, it still needs to prove that it can grow even when crypto isn't breaking records. For now, the move to buy back shares is the biggest tool the company has to try and save the stock from falling further.

Is Robinhood Stock a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Robinhood stock (HOOD) based on 14 Buy and two Hold ratings. The average 12-month HOOD stock price target of $123.64 indicates 68.45% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 3/25/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday amid falling oil prices and new developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Both sides have outlined proposals to end the war, though key differences still remain. A senior Iranian official noted that the country's first reaction to the terms was "not positive," while a top U.S. official characterized Iran's demands as "ridiculous and unrealistic." Still, investors view these actions as a first step in securing a ceasefire.

Iran Sets Ceasefire Conditions, Vows Strikes Until They are Met

Iran laid out five key conditions to end the conflict and said that it would continue missile and drone strikes until they are met. These include war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and a full stop to "aggression and assassinations" by the U.S. and Israel.

Meanwhile, senior Iranian officials denied President Trump's claim of direct peace talks between the two sides and accused him of manipulating the oil and stock markets. Earlier this week, Trump called off an attack on the country's power plants for five days to allow for negotiations.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: New Analyst Coverage Sends Figma (FIG) Stock Lower
data.description:
Shares of Figma (FIG) are slightly down at the time of writing after Oppenheimer (OPY) started covering the stock with a Hold rating. The investment firm sees Figma as a leader in digital design software, with a strong product and clear value for customers. In fact, four-star analyst Brian Schwartz pointed to its fast growth, which has already reached a $1.22 billion revenue run rate. In addition, its web-based platform already includes AI features and is widely used by large companies for design, collaboration, and project management. This helps it stand out in the market.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That said, there are also worries about how AI could affect Figma going forward. While the company has built a strong position through its technology, Schwartz believes that the shift toward AI could slow down customer growth and reduce how much clients spend. More importantly, newer AI-driven tools and "agentic" automation could lower the need for traditional design platforms, while also increasing competition from AI-first companies.

As a result, Figma's competitive edge could become weaker over time. Nevertheless, the overall outlook is not clearly negative. Schwartz estimates that Figma's total market opportunity is about $33 billion, with only a few competitors at its scale, which supports its long-term potential. However, the stock is already trading at a high valuation, around 9 times forward revenue, suggesting that strong expectations are already priced in.

Is FIG Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on FIG stock based on three Buys, seven Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average FIG price target of $40.25 per share implies 86.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
